rules.reason: Enter long when price closes or opens above the central pivot (pivot column); exit when price closes back below pivot; filter for trending regimes only.
Buy when price crosses above or stays above 5-day SMA (trend confirmation), exit when price crosses below SMA; AUTHOR_SILENT exit replaced with mean-reversion reversal logic.
Buy when stock opens below pivot, holds above S1 support, and closes higher on intraday retracement; exit if price breaks below S1 support.

entry_long.description: Price bar shows bullish reversal pattern (higher close after extended trend)
Pin bar or engulfing pattern detected
RSI confirms upward direction (signal magnitude exceeds midpoint threshold)
MACD histogram positive, reinforcing upside confirmation

entry_short.description: Price bar shows bearish reversal pattern (lower close after extended trend)
Pin bar or engulfing pattern detected
RSI confirms downward direction (signal magnitude exceeds midpoint threshold)
MACD histogram negative, reinforcing downside confirmation

rules.reason: Entry requires price reversal pattern confirmation via RSI and MACD agreement; exit triggers on support/resistance breach or indicator mean reversion in TRENDING regimes only.
Enters on directional price movement (above/below SMA-20) with elevated volume and ADX confirmation; exits on reversal, loss of directional pressure (low body%), or volume drying up.
Enters on trend confirmation (ADX, price vs MA) plus pullback signal (wick > body) with close reversal back into trend direction; exits on trend structure break (price crosses MA or ADX weakens).

Entry triggers on confirmed trend (ADX>=25) with pullback compression (range < ATR and reduced body %) and low volume conviction; exits when pullback structure breaks symmetry (range expands) or trend reversal occurs (price crosses MA).
Detects trend reversal via failure patterns (springs/upthrusts) at extremes using wick structure, RSI/MACD weakness, and exits on momentum surge beyond prior extreme with volume confirmation.

entry_long.description: Price reverses and closes back above reversal point
Price retraces into original trend (38.2% minimum retracement)
Volume above average confirming momentum
Momentum divergence: RSI confirmation of price reversal

entry_short.description: Price reverses and closes back below reversal point
Price retraces into original trend (61.8% maximum retracement)
Momentum divergence: RSI confirmation of price reversal

exit_long.description: Exit if momentum fades (MACD histogram turns negative)
Trail stop behind minor swing lows

exit_short.description: Exit if momentum fades (MACD histogram turns positive)
Trail stop behind minor swing highs

rules.reason: Continuation trade on failed reversals: enter when price reverses above/below pivot with volume and momentum confirmation; exit on momentum fade or protective stops at swing levels.
Mean reversion fade trades triggered when price extends beyond Bollinger Bands with RSI confirmation and elevated volume, exiting on mean reversion or time decay.

Fade price extensions beyond Keltner channel boundaries (approximated by Bollinger Bands with 2.0 ATR multiplier equivalent) by entering counter-trend when price closes beyond bands and exiting at midline, with stop loss at 2× ATR from entry.
Buy when price crosses above or trades above the 20-period SMA in a trending regime; exit when price closes below the 20MA support line.
Enter when trend is established (ADX>25), gap occurs (open/close reversal), price retraces to pivot support/resistance, and trend resumes (price crosses pivot); exit when trend weakens (ADX<20).

entry_long.description: Wait for pullback to support (20-period MA) before entering long in uptrending gapping market

exit_long.description: Exit long on breakdown of support level

rules.reason: Signal prohibits shorting at gap-down opens after breakdowns; instead defines a long-only strategy waiting for pullback entries in trending markets, with short entries explicitly disabled.

entry_long.description: Price has bounced from 50% retracement and is above pivot support
Original uptrend resumes - price crosses above MA
Strong trend direction confirmed

exit_long.description: Uptrend reversal - exit on MA breakdown
Price falls below pivot support level

rules.reason: Entry triggered when price bounces from 50% retracement (pivot level) and resumes uptrend above MA; exit on trend reversal or support break.
Entry when price breaks above confluence of intraday R1 and recent range highs with support levels below; exits on support loss or overbought conditions in trending regime.
Price crossing above/below moving average triggers trend reversals; exits occur on counter-crossovers in a trending regime.
Entry triggers when price crosses above MA (sma_20 as default) with support confirmation; exit on MA break below; holds up to 8 candles to capture sustained trending move as per signal specification.
SMA(10) crossing SMA(20) with price confirmation and volume triggers context-dependent long/short entries; reversal of crossover signals exits.
Enters long/short on SMA crossover (10 > 50) with price confirmation and volume, exits on reverse crossover; direction determined by crossover direction.
SMA10/SMA20 crossover strategy: buy on upward cross with price confirmation, sell/exit on downward cross in trending markets.

entry_long.description: Rolling mean of log returns is positive

entry_short.description: Rolling mean of log returns is negative

rules.reason: Enter long/short based on sign of rolling mean log returns (momentum); exit when momentum reverses or holding period (5 bars default) expires.
Enter long/short when rolling mean of log returns crosses above/below zero with confirmation persistence, exit on reversal crossover.

entry_long.description: First trading day of January is up
Second trading day of January is up
Third trading day of January is up
Fourth trading day of January is up
Fifth trading day of January is up

exit_long.description: Exit on last trading day of the year

rules.reason: Enter long on the fifth up trading day of January (if all five are positive) and hold until year-end; exit conditions default to end-of-year as AUTHOR_SILENT implies hold entire year.

Volume spike identified when current volume is at least 2x the 20-day average (proxy for observation period); direction is context-dependent (determined by price action), so both long and short entries apply to the same spike condition; exit after 5 days as a default hold period reflecting the signal's association with significant price move endpoints.

Bull spread entry requires confirmation that nearby prices are outperforming (price above MA) in a confirmed uptrend (ADX > 25); exit when trend strength weakens (ADX < 20) or reversal conditions trigger.

Entry triggers when current true_range exceeds the 2-period average true range (approximated by atr_14 as the closest available volatility measure); direction is NEUTRAL so both long and short entries apply; exit uses default 5-day hold since AUTHOR_SILENT.
Buy on 1.96% decline and sell on 2.24% rise following large moves, with fixed 2-day holding period using intraday returns as the volatility trigger.

entry_long.description: Uptrend is in effect
Lower opening occurs
Low must occur first during trading day (low < high confirms intraday movement)
Lower wick dominates upper wick, confirming low occurred before high (buying day pattern)

rules.reason: Enters long after a lower opening in an uptrend only when the low occurs before the high during the day, ruled out by dominant upper wick which signals high-first reversal pattern.

entry_long.description: Longer-term trend confirmation (ADX indicates trending market)
First oversold condition from short-term stochastic after trend signal

entry_short.description: Longer-term trend confirmation (ADX indicates trending market)
First overbought condition from short-term stochastic after trend signal

exit_long.description: Exit when stochastic reaches overbought (reversal signal)

exit_short.description: Exit when stochastic reaches oversold (reversal signal)

rules.reason: Uses ADX (longer-term trend detector) combined with stochastic K crossings (oversold <20 for long, overbought >80 for short) to time entries within trending regimes, with reversal exits when stochastic reaches opposite extreme.
Buy when 10-day SMA is below yesterday's close AND 5-day stochastic K exceeds 50; exit when stochastic K reverses below 50 threshold.
Enter long when SMA 10 crosses above SMA 50, exit when it crosses below; enter short on reverse crossover, filtered to TRENDING regime.
Enter long when price crosses above a moving average (trend turn up); exit when trend reverses (price crosses below MA).

entry_long.description: Price tested lower levels (double bottom formation)
Declining volume confirmation

exit_long.description: Exit if price breaks below double bottom formation low

rules.reason: Double bottom pattern entry when price consolidates near lows with declining volume confirmation; exit on reversal below formation or after 20 days; related security confirmation and breakout alternative cannot be mechanically tested without multi-asset correlation data.
Buy on close crossing above the pivot point (calculated with 5-day lookback); sell on close crossing below the pivot point, with reversals triggering opposite positions.
Trend-following system using ADX for trend establishment and ATR-based reversal threshold (box size) as exit trigger when price changes direction by the minimum move.
Buy when SMA slope rises (price above MA), sell when slope falls (price below MA), with reversal exits and trending regime filter.

entry_long.description: Trend signal occurs (ADX indicates strong trend)
3-day momentum penetrates lower Bollinger Band

exit_long.description: Trend changes from up to down (ADX weakens below threshold)

rules.reason: Enter long only after trend signal (ADX >25) confirms and price closes below lower Bollinger Band; exit when trend reverses (ADX weakens below 20).
Buy options when 20-day historical volatility is near historical lows with neutral price momentum (RSI 40-60), exiting when realized volatility expands significantly above the implied level.
Mean reversion strategy on overbought/oversold conditions (RSI, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic) with exit at mean and 10-day max hold period, targeting ranging regimes.
